title: DARK SOULS™: Prepare To Die™ Edition

notes:
As others have stated: intro video won't work without using various winetricks, and DSFix is only usable if you do: "WINEPREFIX=~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/211420/pfx/ winecfg" and add "dinput8" as an override (remember to disable the in-game antialiasing as well). Resolution adjustments can cause big white screens though they'll eventually go away. Performance is subpar compared to Windows. Asset loading causes major stutters and FPS can dip quite a bit even after everything is loaded. Overall still quite playable, but not ideal due to the difficulty of the game itself.

Tried it on 4.2-1 as well with the same results.
title: DARK SOULS™: Prepare To Die™ Edition

notes:
Had to disable steam overlay to run otherwise it would not start, works fine without dsfix though at a muddy resolution, few stutters as well in the Undead Asylum for me but cleared up when I got to Undead Burg. 

Online features also work, could see and play bloodstains and read player messages after 10-15 minutes of playing on Luxembourg steam download server. 

Controller not read natively due to steam overlay being disabled but can easily be fixed with ps4drv or xboxdrv depending on controller (I used a PS4 controller).

Can use dsfix which enables modding and resolution configuration but requires adding dinput8 string to 'WINEPREFIX=~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/211420/pfx/ winecfg' library in the GUI.
title: DARK SOULS™: Prepare To Die™ Edition

notes: had to rename movWW directory
title: DARK SOULS™: Prepare To Die™ Edition

notes:
Tested using Proton versions 4.2-2, 3.16-4 and 3.7-8 and all combinations of options. Runs the best when using 3.7-8 and using "PROTON_USE_WINED3D9=1 %command%" in the launch option. Still a little bit of stuttering but is more than playable.

From my testing, Proton 4.2-2 and 3.16-4 breaks full screen, PROTON_NO_D3Dx causes menu and gestures menu to not display and the 2 hand to 1 hand swap to no longer function, and PROTON_NO_ESYNC causes more stuttering.
title: DARK SOULS™: Prepare To Die™ Edition

notes: Works well out of the box. If you have no audio output, try switching the audiobackend to ALSA using winetricks / protontricks.

notes:
game works with no tweaks but still poorly (by the game side). however, a mod that fixes many problems of the game (still by the game side) called DsFix is strongly recommended, should you want to install this mod you'll also be required to add to your steam "launch options" for the game the line;

WINEDLLOVERRIDES="dinput8=n,b" %command%
title: DARK SOULS™: Prepare To Die™ Edition

notes:
How To Run Dark Souls Prepare To Die Edition On Linux Using Steam Proton (Graphics and Stuttering Fix)

A simple guide on how to install Dark Souls Prepare To Die Edition on Linux using Steam Proton

Step 1 – Install WineTricks using a package manager. 

Step 2 – Install Dark Souls Prepare To Die Edition using Steam Proton version 4.11-4

Step 3 – Launch the game and disable both anti-aliasing and motion blur.

Step 4 – Download dsfix from the below link.

https://www.nexusmods.com/darksouls/mods/19/?tab=files

Extract the archive and copy the contents to:

Home / .steam / steam / steamapps / common / Dark Souls Prepare to Die Edition / DATA

Step 5 – Hide the mouse cursor from the game.

Open up DSfix.ini

Under Window & Mouse Cursor Options

Edit to disableCursor 1

Save the file.

Step 6 – Add the following to SET LAUNCH OPTIONS

PROTON_USE_WINED3D9=1 WINEDLLOVERRIDES="dinput8=n,b" gamemoderun %command%

Save the setting. 

You can now launch Dark Souls using a resolution of 1080p and reduce the amount of stuttering so that it is playable. 

REMAINING BUGS

Since the intro cinematic movie, Dark Souls title screen and ending cinematic use the the WMV format, they will either appear as black or not play. Luckily they can be skipped.  

For a visual tutorial see https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=bTBEmsF4Q7w
title: DARK SOULS™: Prepare To Die™ Edition
